steps.title: Follow the counting problem
steps.content: The passage says white-headed langurs are hard to count accurately. If researchers cannot reliably tell whether the population is rising, falling, or stable, it becomes harder to judge whether conservation strategies are working.

steps.title: Remove unsupported effects
steps.content: Choice B predicts that other conservationists will adopt a method, which the text does not suggest. Choice C changes the study's scope without addressing the problem. Choice D invents overlooked conservation approaches. The logical consequence is unreliable conclusions about strategy effectiveness. The answer is A.
